title: Multi-factor Behavioral Authentication Using Correlations Enhanced by Neural Network-based Score Fusion
venue: IEEE 19th Annual Consumer Communications & Networking Conference (CCNC)

method.input_representation: Authentication scores based on behavioral correlations between GPS-Activity, Wi-Fi-Activity, and GPS-Wi-Fi pairs.
method.training_strategy: Hold-out splitting (80% training, 20% testing) with Stratified 5-Fold cross-validation on the training set.

evaluation.context: Reported as HTER (half total error rate) using Neural Network-based score fusion on the MITHRA dataset.
Achieved using Neural Network-based score fusion.
Achieved using Neural Network-based score fusion.

evaluation_protocol: Binary classification of genuine users vs imposters using a global model.

reported_limitations: Dependency on simultaneous availability of smartphone and wearable data

usability.usability_issues: Battery consumption mentioned as a factor for future evaluation.

key_contribution: Proposed correlation-based behavioral authentication matching consistency across sensors rather than historical patterns
Implemented a NN-based score fusion to optimize weights of multi-factor authentication scores
main_strength: Requires significantly less preliminary data than traditional template or classification-based behavioral systems
Outperforms conventional classification methods on the same dataset
main_weakness: Lack of active spoofing/presentation attack testing
Requires two separate devices to be operational
insights.notes_for_review: The method demonstrates that behavioral correlation (consistency) can be a more robust feature than historical patterns when dealing with non-ideal or limited data.
